title: Roundtable organizer analysis
description: The organizer's five-dimension structured analysis of all seat answers, produced in Phase 2 before any adjudication. Consumed by the gap-repair round, the judges, and the synthesizer. Agreements/disagreements are derived views of this analysis, not its whole output.

consensus.description: Points all or most seats share — higher-confidence; the orchestrator locks these.

statement.description: The shared point, as a concise standalone claim.

contradictions.description: Points where seats materially conflict in conclusion, approach, or a key claim (not wording).

statement.description: Neutral one-line statement of what is contested.

positions.description: Each seat's position on this contradiction.

partial_coverage.description: Points only some seats addressed — not contested, but unevenly covered.

unique_insights.description: Valuable points raised by a single seat that no other seat contradicted — additive material to carry into synthesis.

seat.description: The one seat that raised it.

blind_spots.description: Necessary aspects of the task that NO seat addressed.

material_gaps.description: True if there are material contradictions, blind spots, or contested unique insights worth a gap-repair round. When false, Phase 3 is skipped.
